creator: wbe@psr.com
text:
Update on 2025.1:

My guess of 1-2 weeks was too optimistic. :)  I'm some 3 levels deep in changes.  Minutes ago, I got through characterizing the solstice/equinox calculations vs. 1900-2037: the equinox times are usually several minutes early of actual and the solstice times are usually late, but in some years (e.g., 2028) the numbers are wrong by 26-27 minutes.  Some easy changes to fequinoxsolstice() should cut the max. error to +/-15 minutes.  [There's also periodicity in the errors of ~17.6 years which I'm guessing is a Jupiter/Saturn conjunction or something, but I don't think code for that would be worth its minor benefit.]

I'm considering adding a table of the actual solstice and equinox times for recent years through 2037 and using sunpos() only for days/years not in the table, since doing that should be cheap and easy.

Anyhow, work continues.  I'll update again when it's close to done and tested.
